meta.title: End Behavior of f(x) = −2x³ + x − 2 | MAT 170 Exam 2

steps.content: Determine the end behavior of:  f(x) = −2x³ + x − 2
steps.narration: Determine the end behavior of f of x equals negative 2 x cubed plus x minus 2.
steps.label: MAT 170 Exam 2 Review

steps.narration: Here is our function: negative 2 x cubed plus x minus 2.

steps.content: Leading Coefficient Test: The end behavior of a polynomial is determined ONLY by its leading term (the term with the highest power of x).
steps.narration: The Leading Coefficient Test tells us that end behavior depends only on the leading term — the term with the highest power of x.
steps.label: Leading Coefficient Test

steps.narration: The leading term is negative 2 x cubed.
The degree is 3, which is odd.
The leading coefficient is negative 2, which is negative.

steps.content:
Odd degree + Negative leading coefficient:
• As x → −∞, f(x) → +∞  (rises to the left)
• As x → +∞, f(x) → −∞  (falls to the right)
steps.narration: The pattern for odd degree with a negative leading coefficient: the graph rises to the left and falls to the right. Think of it as the opposite of a normal cubic.
steps.label: End Behavior Pattern

steps.narration: As x goes to negative infinity, f of x goes to positive infinity. The graph rises on the left.
As x goes to positive infinity, f of x goes to negative infinity. The graph falls on the right.

steps.content:
End behavior:  Rises left, Falls right
As x → −∞, f(x) → +∞
As x → +∞, f(x) → −∞
steps.narration: The end behavior is: rises to the left and falls to the right.
steps.label: Final Answer

steps.narration: Here is the graph of negative 2 x cubed plus x minus 2. You can see it rises on the left and falls on the right, exactly as predicted by the Leading Coefficient Test.
